terrain_notes: The big one. Loon Lake through Barker Pass (sleep stop, mile 103.1) and Stephen Jones Memorial (mile 110.6) to Tahoe City. There is NO crew and NO pacer swap anywhere in this leg — once you start at Loon Lake you are with me until Tahoe City. I'll sleep at Barker Pass partway through; plan to rest there too. This is two nights and 42 miles. Only take this leg if you can cover the distance and handle broken sleep.
what_to_bring: GPX of course on your own device
Warm layers — gets cold up high
Sun protection — daylight in the back half of this leg
Extra calories and water for long stretches between aids

terrain_notes: Biggest single climb on the back half — 3,476 ft up. Sunday afternoon into evening. Sleep stop at Brockway after. This section can cook in the heat; there are exposed stretches especially in the back half of the climb.
what_to_bring: Water for a long, hot climb

terrain_notes: Mostly down (~3,142 ft loss). Sunday night into Monday morning. Quads may be angry after the Brockway climb. One stream crossing that's usually passable on rocks during race week.
what_to_bring: Trekking poles if you use them

terrain_notes: The final push, and the second-longest leg. Village Green through Spooner Summit (mile 182.3, no crew or pacer swap) to the finish at Heavenly. Two big climbs: 3,716 ft up to Spooner, then 3,294 ft up to the finish ridge. Starts pre-dawn Monday. The Spooner-to-finish stretch plays tricks on the mind — runnable but it feels endless. Your job is to keep the wheels on and get me to the buckle. Like Leg A, there is no legal swap point in this leg: once you start at Village Green you're with me to the finish.
what_to_bring: Caffeine for you — long pre-dawn grind
Calories and water for a long stretch with no crew
